target: Corrected patches for EXP-450 P2 (delete wrong EM-physics paragraph from ring-magnet subsection) and P6 (fix 'in Part 2 alone' scope mismatch). These supersede the corresponding patches in EXP-450.
integration_note: These are corrections to proposed patches in EXP-450. When integrating EXP-450: (1) for P2, use the corrected_p2_html from this EXP instead of EXP-450's P2 replacement_html_block; (2) for P6, use the corrected replacement_html_insertion from this EXP instead of EXP-450's P6 version. EXP-450 P0, P1, P3, P4, P5 are unaffected by this EXP.

patches.description: Insert corrected subsection 2.14 ring-magnet. Deletes the original 3rd paragraph from EXP-450 P2 that incorrectly claimed magnetic dipole decay is '1/r-type (or 1/r^2 in spherically symmetric cases)'. Magnetic dipole far-field decays as 1/r^3, not 1/r or 1/r^2. The paragraph's EM physics was wrong and would hand dome defenders a valid technical rebuttal. Paragraphs 1-2 (topology contradiction, 1660:1 magnitude mismatch) and paragraph 4 (why foundational) carry the argument without needing the EM decay-form paragraph.
patches.replacement_html_block:
2.14 The 1,660:1 Ring-Magnet vs. Fitted-Equation Mismatch

The dome's V51 architecture is topologically a ring magnet: aetheric flux exits the Axis Mundi, traverses the disc, loops through the sump, returns. Flux conservation in any closed magnetic circuit forces near-equal pole strengths. The dome's own ring geometry predicts Bsouth ≈ 39 nT; the dome's fitted equation gives 64,852 nT — a 1,660:1 mismatch between the dome's topology and the dome's own curve-fit.

The geometric prediction. WIN-053 establishes that the V51 aetheric routing — Axis Mundi out, disc traversal, Antarctic barrier descent at r ≈ 20,015 km, sub-terrestrial return through the Sump — is a closed circuit. Closed magnetic circuits obey flux conservation Φ = B·A around the loop. The Axis Mundi aperture (estimated radius ~500 km, generously ~1,000 km) has area Apole ≈ 3.1 × 106 km²; the Antarctic-barrier circumferential band (r = 20,015 km, height ≈ firmament thickness) has area Asouth many orders of magnitude larger. Conservation demands Bsouth/Bpole = Apole/Asouth — yielding a southern-pole field of order 39 nT under the dome's own ring geometry.

The dome's fitted equation contradicts the dome's topology. The dome publishes a fitted B(r) decay equation that produces Bsouth ≈ 64,852 nT at the Antarctic barrier — a value 1,660 times larger than the dome's own ring topology allows. Either the topology is wrong (no closed ring; one-end-open monopole reverting to the dome's rejected V50.6 model) or the fitted equation is wrong (curve-fit to observations, not derived from dome geometry). The dome cannot claim both.

Why this is foundational. The dome's V51 marketing centers on the two-pole geomagnetic architecture as an improvement over V50.6's monopole. Recognizing that a single pole cannot give a dipolar field shows real physical reasoning — this is the kernel of truth in WIN-053. But the execution fails on the dome's own terms: the ring topology and the fitted decay equation disagree by a factor of 1,660. The dome's own published numbers cannot both stand. See WIN-053 for the full flux-conservation derivation and the published 64,852 nT fit.

patches.description: Corrected P6 insertion: changes 'in Part 2 alone' to 'identified in this review'. The original P6 replacement_html_insertion says 'any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model' but {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}}=20 counts ALL review self-contradictions across all parts, not just Part 2. Substituting '20 self-contradictions in Part 2 alone' is numerically wrong (Part 2 contains ~12 named derivations, not 20 verdict-tagged WINs). Fix: change scope qualifier to 'identified in this review'.
patches.find_in_p6_replacement: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions in Part 2 alone falsifies the model
patches.corrected_text: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions identified in this review falsifies the model
patches.full_corrected_insertion: A note on argument abundance. The case below is overdetermined: any one of the {{SELF_CONTRADICTED}} self-contradictions identified in this review falsifies the model, and the External Falsification section adds further independent lines of evidence. A critic who patches any single hole finds the model still falls on twenty others. We engage with every claimed WIN not because we need to, but because the dome author deserves a complete accounting.
